title: podman --connection not working on macOS (failed to resolve active destination: service destination not found)
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

running `podman --remote --connection <connection-string> <some-command>` with the Podman MacOS client errors with `Error: failed to resolve active destination: "<connection-string>" service destination not found`

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Server(s): Podman 3.0.1 on Fedora 33 ([prepared](https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-clients-macos-windows) for remoting)
Client: Podman 3.0.1 on MacOS 11.2.2 x86_64 (installed via homebrew) 

On the Mac client:

1. create two connections:
```
$ podman system connection add conA --identity /Users/myuser/.ssh/podman_rsa ssh://root@servA/run/user/0/podman/podman.sock
$ podman system connection add conB --identity /Users/myuser/.ssh/podman_rsa ssh://root@servB/run/user/0/podman/podman.sock
$ podman system connection default conA
```

2. default connection works (without `--connection`):

```
$ podman --remote info
...
hostname: servA
...
```

3.  trying to use same connection with `--connection <connection-string>` fails:

```
$ podman --remote --connection conA info
Error: failed to resolve active destination: "conA" service destination not found
```

(same for the other connection, also same without `--remote`)


**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ podman --remote --connection conA info
Error: failed to resolve active destination: "conA" service destination not found
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
$ podman --remote --connection conA info
host:
  arch: amd64
...
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

- `--remote --connection <connection-string>` to the same servers seems to work when remoting from a Linux host.
- did not test on Windows

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version
Client:
Version:      3.0.1

Go Version:   go1.15.8
Built:        Sat Feb 20 05:46:59 2021

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

- server: qemu vm
- client: physical (Macbook Intel)

comments.body:
> The root socket listens on /run/podman/podman.sock

@Luap99 thanks for the comment. On my server(s) both exist (`/run/podman/podman.sock` and `/run/user/0/podman/podman.sock`) and I have the same problem with both.

on a Mac:
```
$ podman system connection ls
Name    Identity                          URI
conC*     /Users/myuser/.ssh/podman_rsa  ssh://root@servC:22/run/podman/podman.sock
$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                 COMMAND         CREATED       STATUS           PORTS   NAMES
$ podman --remote -c conC ps
Error: failed to resolve active destination: "conC" service destination not found
```
Can you paste the output of `podman --remote -c conC  --log-level debug ps`.

> Can you paste the output of podman --remote -c conC --log-level debug ps.

@Luap99 

```
$ podman --remote -c conC --log-level debug ps
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Called ps.PersistentPreRunE(podman --remote -c conC --log-level debug ps)
Error: failed to resolve active destination: "conC" service destination not found
```

quick addition: also seeing this on Windows (Podman 3.0.1 via MSI installer) on Win10-2004 AMD64)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6320032/109419399-903b6400-79cd-11eb-9846-515bbbdba55a.png)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@christian-korneck Are you still seeing this issue.  @ashley-cui @baude Have been working hard on improving Podman on MAC.

> Are you still seeing this issue. 

yes, still seeing this issue after having upgraded Podman client (Mac/Win) and server (fc 33) to **`3.1.0`**. Both with existing and recreated connection. Haven't tried from `main`. 

> @ashley-cui @baude Have been working hard on improving Podman on MAC.

thanks for bringing Podman to Mac/Win, it's already super useful!!
I'll poke this a bit today
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@ashley-cui Did you make any progress on this?
Sorry, dropped off my list. Seems like still a problem, I will try to get to it this week.
